response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and has built low-latency, high-throughput systems in DLang with async I/O and fibers, which maps well to systems-level pipeline work. However, modern C++ exposure is thin and dated (a brief 2014-2015 stint at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis), there is no Rust, and recent roles have been TypeScript/Node full-stack product work. There is also no direct navigation/routing pipeline experience, so the fit hinges on transferable systems skills rather than demonstrated domain expertise.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of an open-source GIS project used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec) using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and Linux/DevOps familiarity
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and nearly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct experience with routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipelines
Recent years focused on TypeScript/Node web product work, away from systems programming
